title: Tahini Sauce
intro:
Tahini Sauce

Tahini sauce is the classic companion to falafel across the Eastern Mediterranean and the Levant. From Cairo street carts to Jerusalem falafel stands, warm chickpea fritters are almost always finished with a drizzle of this pale sesame sauce. The foundation is simple: sesame tahini, lemon, garlic, and water. The technique is what makes it special. When properly emulsified the sauce turns lighter in color, creamy, and almost fluffy. That texture lets it coat falafel without making the pita soggy.

stepGroups.title: Make the garlic paste
items: Place the garlic cloves on the cutting board and smash them firmly with the flat side of your chef’s knife to crush. Sprinkle the salt over the smashed garlic. Using the flat side of the knife, press and drag the blade across the garlic while holding the edge slightly off the board. The salt acts as an abrasive and quickly grinds the garlic into a smooth paste. Continue smearing and scraping until the garlic becomes a sticky, uniform paste with no visible pieces.

stepGroups.title: Bloom the garlic
items: Add the lemon juice and stir. Let it sit for about 1 minute. This softens the harsh edge of the raw garlic and distributes the flavor through the sauce.

stepGroups.title: Add the tahini
items: Stir in the tahini. The mixture will immediately become thick and slightly pasty. This is normal.

stepGroups.title: Create the falafel-shop emulsion
items: Begin whisking while slowly adding the ice-cold water. At first the mixture will tighten and look almost broken. Keep whisking. As more water goes in, the sauce will suddenly loosen and lighten in color. This is the sesame emulsion forming.

stepGroups.title: Aerate the sauce
items: For the best texture, run an immersion stick blender in the bowl for about 10 seconds once all the water is added. This aerates the sesame emulsion and produces the pale, creamy sauce you see at good falafel stands.

stepGroups.title: Finish the sauce
items: Stir in the chopped parsley and ground cumin.

stepGroups.title: Adjust the consistency
items: The finished sauce should pour easily but still coat a spoon. If it feels too thick, whisk in 1 tablespoon cold water at a time until it drizzles smoothly.
